human_verification.notes: Reviewed government reporting boundary, statutory authority awareness, official-record traceability, due-process escalation, procurement and public-records boundaries, and validated specification-based posture.

review_notes: Scenario suite covers normal reporting, metric disputes, stale and conflicting official records, incomplete submission inputs, unsupported disclosures, tenant-specific adaptation, eligibility or rights-sensitive exceptions, missing audit trail, public-records/privacy routing, and adjudication/enforcement refusal.
All required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and government-and-public-sector abilities are mapped to passing scenarios.
Research summary covers required public source classes and domain scenario families with no blocking unresolved ambiguity.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary controls prevent legal, adjudication, enforcement, procurement-award, public-records-release, audit-opinion, and financial-approval overreach while preserving reporting-lane authority.
All reporting outputs require source tie-outs, metric-definition handling, official-record lineage, caveats, and uncertainty markers.
Research and scenarios prove eligibility, records, public-service workflow, due-process, and auditability constraints are represented.
Deployment notes define required tenant artifacts, regulated-lane caveats, rollback behavior, and materialization prerequisites.
notes: Runtime remains specification-based and should be promoted to trusted built only with explicit governance approval and a separate committed runtime package.
Tenant-supplied jurisdiction, statute, program policy, source-precedence, records policy, retrieval hooks, and authority-owner mappings are required for high-confidence outputs.
